name: Livery Name

sections.body: This is where the creative-fiction origin story lives — the why/how/when behind the build, told with some license. Doesn't need to be literally true, just true to the car.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: This is where technique commentary lives — how it was actually built in the livery editor, material/layering choices, anything the tools fought you on.

name: Smokin

sections.body: [Dummy copy] Built late one night after losing a Stunt Park lobby in something far less subtle, this one was about proving restraint could still look mean. The olive and gold were picked to feel like a getaway car that's already won the chase before it started — quiet until it isn't.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: Confirmed via the Forza Wiki: it's the 2007 Formula Drift #117 599 GTB Fiorano — nicknamed "Fiorella" — built by Federico Sceriffo, with twin positive-displacement superchargers (not turbos) pushing the V12 to roughly 917 hp, a Hollinger 6-speed sequential replacing the stock semi-automatic F1 transmission, Öhlins coilovers, and at 3,208 lb it's the heaviest car in Formula Drift's Forza roster. Front-mid-engine layout gives it a rear-biased 47/53 weight split; custom control arms and wide fenders were fitted specifically to increase steering angle — the whole point of the car.
sections.label: Design Notes

subtitle: Ferrari 599 GTB Fiorano (Formula Drift 599D) · matte olive & gold pinstripe · Drift

name: Lolita

sections.body: Lolita is set up with rally tires and suspension. I'll have to get back to you on the tuning, as that's a next-tier initiative to include specific tunings that go with the cars. But the idea with this setup is that it can do most everything. It can drift pretty well — rally tires are, in my opinion, the second-best for drifting — and obviously they give improved speed and traction on the dirt road speed zones. Basically, it's a pretty good car to do Stunt Party and many of the PR Events. It's not the fastest possible, but it definitely will test your limits of control.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: Built on the Mazda MX-5 Miata Forza Edition shell — stock, that's a V10-swapped drag special. Lolita's been pulled in a completely different direction: converted to AWD, dropped onto rally rubber. The damascus-steel wrap reads almost like a topographic map at a glance, which is exactly why it took a second look to realize it wasn't one — the rippling forge-pattern and contour lines share that same swirling logic.
sections.label: Design Notes
Tune / Build Parts

subtitle: Mazda MX-5 Miata FE · Damascus Steel · Rally · Rims: BBS RS Turbofan

name: Tune In Tokyo

sections.body: [Dummy copy] Imagined as the car that never makes it home before the lights come back on — built for the loop between Shibuya crossing reruns and a back-alley meet that only exists because three friends agreed to text the location at the same time. The sunburst is the moment the camera flash actually lands.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: [Dummy copy] The neon gradient was built in layers from the inside out, exposed-engine cutaway included partly for shock value and partly because the underlying widebody kit left room to show it off. Turbo placement dictated where the brightest colors could actually sit without clipping into panel gaps.
sections.label: Design Notes

subtitle: Roadster · neon sunburst wrap · turbo widebody

name: Photo Safari: Discover Japan

sections.body: [Dummy copy] Started as a joke about how serious every other build in this catalog had gotten. The smiley face was supposed to be temporary — a placeholder livery for a single screenshot — and then it just never came off, because something about a trophy truck grinning through a mountain pass was funnier every time it got photographed again.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: [Dummy copy] The smiley itself is a built-in decal, not hand-painted, scaled up to cover most of the rear quarter panel. The challenge was less the face and more keeping the rest of the livery quiet enough that the one goofy element actually reads as the point rather than competing with anything else going on. mouse
sections.label: Design Notes

subtitle: Off-road trophy truck · novelty smiley wrap

name: Violet

sections.body: [Dummy copy] Named for the color before anything else about it existed — the cloud pattern, the green wheels, even the car choice came after deciding the only non-negotiable was that exact shade of violet showing up under stadium lights at night.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: [Dummy copy] Cloud shapes were built freehand rather than pulled from a preset pattern, which took longer but kept the edges soft in a way the built-in cloud decals don't quite manage. Green wheels were chosen specifically to clash on purpose against the violet rather than complement it.
sections.label: Design Notes

subtitle: Toyota GR Supra · violet cloud wrap · green forged wheels

name: A Better A-Team

sections.body: Although B.A. Baracus famously feared flying, this van can catch plenty of air and look badass doin' it! Upgraded carbon fiber body and authentic bullet holes in the rear panel — you'll have plenty of muscle to get away from the bad guys, and with that mean stance, you'll look like you're going a hundred miles an hour standing still.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: Built on the GMC Vandura shell as a direct callback to the actual A-Team van, but pushed further than the show ever did: the roof gets a carbon-fiber texture instead of plain sheet metal, the red pinstripe is sharpened into a single clean diagonal rather than the original's slightly softer line, and the wheels are swapped to a red-lipped aftermarket style instead of the steelies the real van wore. The rear panel carries "Pity the fool" in script — a Mr. T line that never actually appeared on the van itself, added here as the kind of detail a fan would want but the show never gave them.
sections.label: Design Notes

subtitle: GMC Vandura · Matte Black & Carbon Roof · Street · Rims: Fifteen52 Forged 3PC Formula TR

name: Space Buggy

sections.body: I drove this little buggy all over Mexico, up and down the volcano and from the sand dunes to jungles of the south. It's super fun to drive and make great donuts. It can even hold its own in Cross Country racing!
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: Using the swirly anti-spying paint job on the buggy's bumper bars, I wanted the craziest wheels, so the mix of teal and the lunar lander like look of those racing wheels, I was able to give it a future is now vibe.
sections.label: Design Notes

subtitle: Ariel Nomad This needs to have areas that persist

sections.body: Took Lolita down to the lake to get some fun in the sun. We made a splash, and reflected on our inner beauty. But ultimately it's our outer beauty that's most enamoring. Now baptized in the zen waters of a placid Japanese lake, listening to Takehiro Sato and Opus Radio, we find ourselves refreshed and ready for another skill song, the drudgery of our last Raku Taku shift washed away with the lady in the lake.
sections.label: Inspiration

sections.body: The rally tires are quite appropriate for this setting. They look great, all wet and reflected in the undulating surface of the lake. Too bad these pictures all have to be stills. The evening sun lent some nice golden hour warmth to contrast the cool tones which make Lolita seem so neon when in the city at night. The lake and the light suit her curves and make Lolita sparkle! Gold and silver and covered in lipstick. Throw in the whirlwind for good measure and you've got an almost out of control little hot one on your hands. Be careful, it might be too hot to touch!
